hazard.summary: 429 handler in generateVariant() ignored Retry-After header, causing retries inside provider cooldown window using only local exponential backoff.
signals: 429 handler detected rate limiting but did not inspect Retry-After
Retry delay controlled by local exponential schedule (2s/4s/8s)
Fix merged in PR #1337 via commit 00f966b
Closed as completed in GStack v1.30.0.0 by garrytan

impact.operator_impact: Batch image generation workloads burned retry attempts inside provider cooldown windows, degrading throughput and wasting quota.
impact.business_consequence: Variable execution cost increases under concurrent load when provider cooldown windows are ignored by local retry scheduling.

constraints: Retry-After headers must take precedence over locally computed retry schedules when present.
429 retry logic must preserve provider cooldown semantics across batch execution paths.
Generated workflow code must inherit the same retry semantics as pool-level cooldown systems.
verification: Setup: Configure image generation workflow where provider returns 429 with Retry-After. Action: Execute generateVariant() under batch load. Assertion: Retry scheduling honors Retry-After before local backoff.
Setup: Simulate cooldown windows longer than local retry schedule. Action: Trigger repeated generation attempts. Assertion: No retries occur before cooldown expires.

constraints: Provider cooldown signals must override local retry timing.
Retry decisions must remain synchronized with provider cooldown windows.
anti_patterns: local retry policy overriding provider cooldown semantics
batch retries continuing during known cooldown periods

notes: Highest-profile confirmed fix in corpus. Merged by Garry Tan (YC co-founder) in GStack v1.30.0.0 as part of 21-PR community fix wave. Issue filed by SirBrenton with corpus reference. Fix implemented RFC 7231 compliant Retry-After parsing with 60s cap.
